etymology.description: phonetic 𧮫 (jué) + semantic 卩 (seal) -> que sound with idea of sealing, blocking -> however
etymology.interpretation: The character 卻 (què, 'however') combines the phonetic element 𧮫 (jué) to indicate the pronunciation and the semantic element 卩 (seal) which traditionally conveys the idea of sealing or blockading. This conveys the sense of blocking or negating expectations, resulting in the meaning 'however'.
etymology.simplification: 却 is a simplified variant of 卻, retaining the phonetic and semantic elements while reducing stroke complexity.

examples.chinese: 他努力工作(他努力工作)，却未能成功(卻未能成功)。
examples.pinyin: Tā nǔlì gōngzuò, què wèinéng chénggōng.
examples.english: He worked hard, however, he couldn't succeed.

examples.chinese: 我很累(我很累)，却无法入睡(卻無法入睡)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ hěn lèi, què wúfǎ rùshuì.
examples.english: I am very tired, however, I can't fall asleep.

examples.chinese: 她答应了(她答應了)，却没有出现(卻沒有出現)。
examples.pinyin: Tā dāyìng le, què méiyǒu chūxiàn.
examples.english: She agreed, however, she didn't show up.
